name: Elm.Declaration.Diff
comment:
Diff a declaration

@docs aliasesAreEquivalent, unionsAreEquivalent, valuesAreEquivalent

values.name: unionsAreEquivalent
values.comment:
Are both [`Elm.Docs.Union`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/elm/project-metadata-utils/latest/Elm-Docs#Union) type declarations equal?

Reminder: Emulates `elm diff` behaviour and therefore explicitly e.g. says exposing the variants necessitates a major version bump.

name: Elm.Docs.Diff
comment:
Diff a list of [`Elm.Docs.Module`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/elm/project-metadata-utils/latest/Elm-Docs#Module)s.

@docs Diff, for, toMagnitude

aliases.name: Diff
aliases.comment:
Names of removed and added modules
and an [`Elm.Module.Diff.Diff`](Elm-Module-Diff#Diff) for all modules that were changed.
aliases.type: { modulesAdded : Set.Set String.String, modulesChanged : Dict.Dict String.String Elm.Module.Diff.Diff, modulesRemoved : Set.Set String.String }

values.name: for
values.comment: A [`Diff`](#Diff) with everything that changed from `old -> new`
values.type: { old : List.List Elm.Docs.Module, new : List.List Elm.Docs.Module } -> Elm.Docs.Diff.Diff

aliases.name: Diff
aliases.comment:
For union `type`, `type alias` and function/value declarations:
Names of removed and added declarations and both declarations if a declaration was changed.
aliases.type: { unions : { added : Dict.Dict String.String Elm.Docs.Union, changed : Dict.Dict String.String { old : Elm.Docs.Union, new : Elm.Docs.Union }, removed : Dict.Dict String.String Elm.Docs.Union }, aliases : { added : Dict.Dict String.String Elm.Docs.Alias, changed : Dict.Dict String.String { old : Elm.Docs.Alias, new : Elm.Docs.Alias }, removed : Dict.Dict String.String Elm.Docs.Alias }, values : { added : Dict.Dict String.String Elm.Docs.Value, changed : Dict.Dict String.String { old : Elm.Docs.Value, new : Elm.Docs.Value }, removed : Dict.Dict String.String Elm.Docs.Value } }

values.name: for
values.comment: A [`Diff`](#Diff) with everything that changed from `old -> new`

name: Elm.SemanticMagnitude
comment:
Severity of a diff

@docs Magnitude, theMoreSevere, name

unions.name: Magnitude
unions.comment:
What kind of version bump would be necessary?

  - `Major`: stuff has been deleted or a type has changed
  - `Minor`: stuff has been added and the types still fit
  - `Patch`: no API changes, either implementation or documentation changes

values.name: name
values.comment:
Lowercase name

    "-- This is a "
        ++ (Elm.SemanticMagnitude.Minor |> Elm.SemanticMagnitude.name |> String.toUpper)
        ++ " change. --"
    --> "-- This is a MINOR change. --"

values.name: theMoreSevere
values.comment:
Choose maximum severity between the two [`Magnitude`](#Magnitude)s

    [ Elm.SemanticMagnitude.Minor
    , Elm.SemanticMagnitude.Major
    , Elm.SemanticMagnitude.Patch
    ]
        |> List.foldl
            (\next soFar -> ( soFar, next ) |> Elm.SemanticMagnitude.theMoreSevere)
            Elm.SemanticMagnitude.Patch
    --> Elm.SemanticMagnitude.Major

values.name: areEquivalent
values.comment:
Are both [`Elm.Type.Type`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/elm/project-metadata-utils/latest/Elm-Type#Type)s equal?

Put `parameters = []` for value/function/port declaration types
otherwise provide the variable names after the declared name for `type`/`type alias` declarations.
values.type: ( { type_ : Elm.Type.Type, parameters : List.List String.String }, { type_ : Elm.Type.Type, parameters : List.List String.String } ) -> Basics.Bool

values.name: variable
values.comment:
Reminder: Emulates `elm diff` behaviour and therefore explicitly emulates its errors:

  - removing all constraints is considered a patch change,
    even though it removed capabilities and will break your code on upgrade

  - changing constrained variables always trigger a major version bump, with the only exception being
    `number -> comparable` which _should_ actually be major.

  - you will not see minor magnitudes as results because elm considers
    even e.g. `variable -> number` a patch change, even though the resulting type gives you more possibilities.
